acceptance.proof: Both bound driver sources validate from complete LF and byte-equivalent complete CRLF materializations against the unchanged canonical LF SHA-256 values. The checker reads the current working-tree bytes rather than trusting a Git blob, so uncommitted source mutations remain visible.

The focused checker deterministically rejects mixed LF/CRLF, lone carriage returns, and a non-line-ending mutation of either source. Canonicalization changes only CRLF pairs to LF and never trims, decodes, rewrites, or normalizes other bytes.

The provenance manifest adds an explicit canonical-LF digest basis to the two existing driver bindings while retaining both SHA-256 values and every accepted case, observation, raw artifact digest, normalization operation, external identity, limitation, and frozen historical digest unchanged.

The complete source-only gate passes in the Windows CRLF PowerShell working-tree materialization that reproduced the public CI failure, and the same focused canonicalization checks cover LF materialization without requiring Git configuration, network access, or an oracle rerun.

The Rust crate, Cargo graph, feature lock, project specification, effect union, oracle drivers, and runtime surfaces remain unchanged, dependency-free, and inert. No implementation source is inspected or copied and no LSL, device, adapter, provider, or Manifold authority behavior is added.

The corrective failure, exact bounded repair, public evidence limits, and reviewed instruction surfaces are recorded through the portable workflow without hand-editing lifecycle state or erasing the failed public-main CI attempt.

commit_policy: Commit the portable LSLC-001H driver-provenance validation repair separately from any candidate XML, StreamDefinition, runtime, protocol, dependency, oracle-execution, adapter, provider, or feature work.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the checkout-portable canonical driver-source digest and its strict mixed/lone-CR and content-mutation boundary.
Describe the failed Windows checkout validation, bounded repair, unchanged observation evidence, and limits of the portability claim.

Review whether checkout materialization versus canonical provenance requires general evidence guidance; retain the skill unchanged if its exact-revision and failure-preservation rules already cover the repair.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review whether public clean-source oracle provenance needs new cross-checkout routing; retain the skill unchanged if the current public-boundary and validation rules already cover it.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

non_scope: Any change to crates/rusty-lsl, Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock, the feature lock, project specification, public Rust API, XML candidate policy, StreamDefinition mapping, parser, serializer, protocol, wire, discovery, inlet, outlet, transport, clock, buffering, runtime, adapter, provider, FFI, device, or publication behavior.

Rerunning or modifying the pinned oracle, changing either oracle driver source, acquiring packages, loading native libraries, opening networking, creating outlets or inlets, inspecting liblsl, rLSL, pylsl, wrapper, application, test, build, vendored, generated-protocol, or source-repository implementation material, or using any such source as implementation input.

Rewriting the accepted LSLC-001H observation overlay, case manifest, raw-capture hashes, artifact identities, normalized public XML, runtime placeholders, capture window, limitations, accepted validation receipt, or historical frozen corpus and fixture bytes.

Generic newline normalization for arbitrary repository artifacts, acceptance of bare carriage returns or mixed newline conventions, Git-index-only validation that could ignore uncommitted source changes, weakening content mutation detection, or treating semantic equivalence as authority to change captured behavior.

Manifold stream admission, registry, subscriptions, routes, leases, revisions, replay, identity, authorization, audit, Morphospace-native transport, Quest or Hostess behavior, platform lifecycle, product policy, or commands derived from samples.
objective: Repair the accepted LSLC-001H source-validation gate so the two provenance-bound text drivers validate identically from complete LF or complete CRLF Git working-tree materializations while preserving the canonical LF source digest, rejecting mixed or lone-carriage-return line endings and every non-line-ending content mutation, retaining all accepted oracle observations and external evidence unchanged, and adding no candidate LSL behavior, runtime effect, dependency, source inspection, or authority transfer.
outputs: A focused canonical text-source digest helper used only for the two committed LSLC-001H oracle driver bindings. It accepts a complete LF file or the byte-equivalent complete CRLF materialization, hashes canonical LF bytes, rejects mixed LF/CRLF and lone carriage returns, and does not normalize any other byte.

Deterministic in-memory focused checks proving LF/CRLF digest equivalence for the actual bound sources, rejection of mixed and lone-CR forms, and digest inequality after a non-line-ending source mutation.

An additive provenance binding basis for both driver entries that names canonical LF source bytes without changing the existing SHA-256 values, driver contents, oracle identity, observations, raw evidence, normalization records, or accepted capture facts.
Public validation and provenance routing that preserves GitHub Actions run 29276386135 as the failed pre-fix integration attempt and limits the repair claim to checkout-portable source validation.
